steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has an independent clause ending with 'lists' followed by a long elaboration naming specific examples. The question asks which punctuation correctly connects them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — lists: in: A colon after a complete independent clause correctly introduces an elaboration or list. ✓
  • Choice B — lists in: Running the independent clause straight into the elaboration without any punctuation creates a run-on. ✗
  • Choice C — lists in:: Placing the colon after the preposition 'in' breaks an incomplete construction. ✗
  • Choice D — lists, in: A comma is too weak here; a colon is required to signal that an elaboration follows. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
